regarding the transfer.
Government of Maharashtra

Government Order No.TRF-0126 / Q.No.5 / Aastha-2 (E-1522787)
Madam Cama Marg, Martyrdom Rajguru Chowk,
Mantralaya, Mumbai 400 032.
Date: 30th March, 2026
Reference: - 1. Regulation of transfers and duties of Government of Maharashtra Government employees.
The Prevention of Delay in Execution Act, 2005
General Administration Department Notification No. SRV-2021 / Q. No.49 / Works-
Government Orders: -
Regulation of transfers and discharge of government duties of Maharashtra Government employees.

As per the provisions of Section 4 (4) and 4 (5) of the Prevention of Delay Act, 2005, the following officer of the cadre of Deputy Collector shall, for administrative reasons, be posted in front of his name in column No.
in the interest of public service with the approval of the competent authorities in the position indicated in section 4 and
The posting is being done for administrative convenience.
2. Date of joining of the office of the Deputy Collector mentioned above.
Unilateral discharge from the present working post from 30.03.2026 (M.No.). Related to
The officer should upload a copy of the Appointment Report / CTC https://maharevenuehr.in on the portal as to the date on which he / she appeared for the new appointment.

The officers concerned shall be such as may be prescribed under Rule 15 of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Period of assumption of office, non-acceptance of service and remuneration during periods of suspension, dismissal and removal from service) Rules, 1981.
As per the provisions, it may be noted that the place of transfer has not been joined before the expiry of the term of office.
If they come, they will be charged under the Representation of the People Act, 1950 and the Maharashtra Civil Services (Conduct) Rules, 1979.
Those concerned should note that disciplinary action will be taken in accordance with the provisions of the Act.

The Maharashtra Civil Services (Period of assumption of office, non-recurring service and remuneration during periods of suspension, dismissal and removal from service) Rules, 1981, relating to the period prescribed under the rules.
The officer should be careful about joining the place of posting. Otherwise, join the position of this post.
If not, the period of their absence will count as a "non-deceiving day."
The concerned should take note.

para_trans: Leave should not be granted to the officer concerned for any reason without the prior approval of the Government.

Also, as per Rule-30 (c) of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1981, no leave applications of officers transferred by the original office shall be entertained. If such an application is received, it shall be
The officers must return the payment by post to the original address of the registrar.
6. Not joining the office of immediate posting under such order or under any kind of pressure.
If an attempt is made to cancel the transfer by bringing, this action is governed by the Maharashtra Civil Services (Conduct) Rules, 1979.
Being in violation of Rule 23, disciplinary action is taken against them as it is misconduct.
The concerned should note that it will be done.
7. Any criminal or other offence of any kind may be registered against the aforesaid officer in future.
If so, a detailed report shall be submitted to the Government immediately by the Divisional Commissioner concerned.
should be done. Also, any departmental inquiry at the field level is initiated against the concerned officers.
The government should be informed immediately if this is the case or if it is initiated.
The Government Order is available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.maharashtra.gov.in.
